message:
fix(term): use saturating_add for sixel y-coordinate increment

In sixel graphics parsing, the y coordinate (u32) is incremented by 6
on each NewLine. While practically impossible to overflow (would require
a >4 billion pixel tall image), using saturating_add prevents undefined
behavior if a malicious sixel stream sends enough newlines.

The existing bounds check at line 47 (`y + bitno >= height`) already
prevents out-of-bounds pixel writes, but saturating_add makes the
overflow safety explicit.

Validated: 261 term tests pass via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
